reasoning: The US Treasury yield curve is upward sloping (normal), which signifies market expectations of future economic expansion [57205ed3e4b4]. Furthermore, real rates near zero typically support higher PE valuation multiples for equities [5e068f4bde46]. While the VIX is currently in an 'elevated' regime, its recent 1-month and 3-month trends are falling, aligning with the bullish pressure from low real rates. The signal agreement is mixed because the current VIX state (elevated) conflicts with the bullish indicators of the yield curve and real rates, although the VIX trend is improving.
